description: AI agent that scrapes Luma event data, generates t-shirt design briefs, self-critiques, creates designs via Gemini, uploads to Fourthwall as products, sets up a storefront, and blasts attendees with the store link via Luma cohost. CLI-based, with Weave observability.

userStories.title: Set up Python project and install dependencies
userStories.description: Initialize the Python project with a virtual environment and install all required packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a virtual environment (venv) if not present
Install packages from requirements.txt: google-genai, openai, weave, wandb, python-dotenv, requests
Create app/__init__.py to make it a package
Verify imports work by running: python -c 'import google.genai; import weave; print("OK")'
Create app/config.py that loads .env file using python-dotenv and exports config values (including FOURTHWALL_API_USERNAME, FOURTHWALL_API_PASSWORD)
Verify playwright-cli is available: playwright-cli --version
userStories.notes: Use .env for all keys. See .env.example for reference. playwright-cli is already installed globally.

userStories.title: Scrape Luma event data using playwright-cli
userStories.description: Use playwright-cli to navigate to the Luma event page and extract event details into a JSON file.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/scrape_event.py with a scrape_event(event_url) function
Use subprocess to call playwright-cli commands: open the event URL, take a snapshot, extract text
Parse the extracted page content to build an EventData dict with: name, description, date, location, sponsors, attendee_types
Save scraped data to data/scraped_event.json
Also keep data/events.json as fallback mock data if scraping fails
Test: python -c 'from app.scrape_event import scrape_event; print(scrape_event("https://luma.com/hh5k4ahp"))'
userStories.notes: Use playwright-cli CLI commands via subprocess. Key commands: playwright-cli open <url>, playwright-cli snapshot, playwright-cli eval <js>. The event page is public so no login needed for scraping. Login creds in .env are for co-host features only (not needed for this task).

userStories.title: Generate 10 design briefs from event data
userStories.description: Use LLM to analyze event data and generate 10 t-shirt design concepts across 3 categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/briefs.py with generate_briefs(event_data) function
Use W&B Inference API (OpenAI client) to generate 10 design briefs
Each brief has: id, title, category (crisp-simple/funny-meme/sponsor-logo), description, slogans list, color_notes
Distribution: 4 crisp-simple, 4 funny-meme, 2 sponsor-logo
Briefs reference event-specific humor (lobster costume rule, Ralph Wiggum origin, autonomous agents theme)
Return list of 10 brief dicts
Test with scraped or mock event data
userStories.notes: Use model 'OpenPipe/Qwen3-14B-Instruct' via W&B Inference. Prompt should ask for JSON output. Sponsor names as text only (no logos). All designs black and white.

userStories.title: Self-critique briefs and narrow to 6
userStories.description: Use LLM to evaluate the 10 briefs and select the best 6 with critique notes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/critique.py with critique_briefs(briefs) function
Use W&B Inference API to evaluate briefs against: printability, relevance, variety, humor/appeal
Return the top 6 briefs with a critique_notes field added to each
Target distribution in final 6: ~2 crisp, ~2 funny, ~2 sponsor (quality over quota)
Test: python -c 'from app.critique import critique_briefs; ...'
userStories.notes: This is the self-critique loop from the design doc. Feed all 10 briefs to the LLM and ask it to rank and select 6.

userStories.title: Generate t-shirt design images with Gemini
userStories.description: Use Gemini image generation to create 6 t-shirt graphics from the selected briefs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/design.py with generate_design(brief) function
Use google-genai library with model 'gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview'
Generate black and white t-shirt graphic for each brief
Prompt emphasizes: high contrast, clean lines, print-ready, white background, centered
Save images to output/ directory as PNG with descriptive filenames
Sequential generation with 2-second delay between calls
Retry up to 2 times on API error
Test: generate at least 1 design successfully
userStories.notes: Use types.GenerateContentConfig(response_modalities=['TEXT', 'IMAGE']). See CLAUDE.md for exact code pattern. Accept whatever Gemini produces if API call succeeds.

userStories.title: Upload designs to Fourthwall and create t-shirt products
userStories.description: Use playwright-cli to automate Fourthwall product creation: log in, upload each design image, configure product details, and publish.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/fourthwall.py with upload_designs(selected_briefs, image_paths) function
Use playwright-cli to log in to Fourthwall (PLATFORM_EMAIL + PLATFORM_PASSWORD from .env)
For each design: navigate to product creation, upload the PNG image, set title from brief, set description, set pricing
Publish each product to the shop
Return list of created product URLs
Save results to checkpoints/05-fourthwall-products.json
Test: successfully create at least 1 product on Fourthwall
userStories.notes: Fourthwall Open API is read-only for products (list/get only). Product creation must use browser automation via playwright-cli. Log in at fourthwall.com, use the product designer UI. Fourthwall API creds (FOURTHWALL_API_USERNAME, FOURTHWALL_API_PASSWORD) can be used to verify products were created via GET /open-api/v1.0/products.

userStories.title: Configure Fourthwall storefront website
userStories.description: Use playwright-cli to set up the Fourthwall storefront with event branding so it's ready to share.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/storefront.py with setup_storefront(event_data, product_urls) function
Use playwright-cli to navigate Fourthwall shop settings
Set shop name to event-themed name (e.g., 'Unpossible Merch - Ralphathon')
Ensure all created products are visible on the storefront
Capture and return the public storefront URL
Save results to checkpoints/06-storefront.json
Test: storefront URL is accessible and shows products
userStories.notes: The storefront may already be partially set up. Focus on ensuring products are visible and capturing the public URL. Use Fourthwall API (GET /open-api/v1.0/shops/current) to verify shop details.

userStories.title: Send Luma blast to attendees with store link
userStories.description: Use playwright-cli to send a blast to all event attendees (as cohost) announcing the merch store with the Fourthwall link.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/luma_blast.py with send_blast(event_url, storefront_url) function
Use playwright-cli to log in to Luma (PLATFORM_EMAIL + PLATFORM_PASSWORD from .env)
Navigate to event > Manage Event > Blasts
Compose blast with: merch announcement, storefront URL, brief description of available designs
Send blast to all 'Going' attendees
Save results to checkpoints/07-luma-blast.json
Test: blast is sent successfully (verify via Luma UI or confirmation message)
userStories.notes: Luma API (https://public-api.luma.com) does not have a blast endpoint. Must use browser automation. The agent is a cohost on the event so has permissions to send blasts. Blast goes to all attendees via email + SMS + push.

userStories.title: Build end-to-end CLI pipeline with checkpoints
userStories.description: Create a main orchestrator that runs all 8 stages sequentially with checkpoint recovery.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/pipeline.py with run_pipeline() function
Stages: scrape event -> generate briefs -> critique to 6 -> generate images -> upload to Fourthwall -> setup storefront -> send Luma blast
Add checkpoint system: each stage saves to checkpoints/ dir as JSON
On restart, skip completed stages by checking checkpoints
Print progress to stdout (which stage, which step)
Save final results summary to output/results.json
Create run.py at project root that calls run_pipeline()
Test: python run.py
userStories.notes: Checkpoint files: 01-event-data.json through 07-luma-blast.json. Add --clean flag to clear checkpoints.

userStories.title: Add Weave tracing to all pipeline functions
userStories.description: Instrument the pipeline with W&B Weave for full observability.
acceptanceCriteria: Add weave.init('saikolapudi-aws/tshirt-gen') at pipeline startup
Decorate key functions with @weave.op: run_pipeline, scrape_event, generate_briefs, critique_briefs, generate_design, upload_designs, setup_storefront, send_blast
Add weave.attributes context for session_id and event_name
Verify traces appear in Weave UI after a test run
Do NOT break existing functionality
userStories.notes: See docs/w&b.md for Weave integration guide. Use @weave.op decorator pattern.

userStories.title: Error handling, polish, and demo script
userStories.description: Add graceful error handling and a demo run script.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap each pipeline stage in try/except with clear error messages
If image generation fails for one brief, continue to next
If Fourthwall upload fails for one product, continue to next
Add --event-url flag to run.py for custom event URL
Add --mock flag to use data/events.json instead of scraping
Add --skip-blast flag to skip sending the Luma blast
Print summary at end: stages completed, designs generated, products created, blast sent, errors
Test: python run.py --mock --skip-blast
userStories.notes: This is polish for demo. Keep it simple. The --mock and --skip-blast flags are important for testing without side effects.
